title: NOC 21102 – Geoscientists and Oceanographers | GTR Immigration
description: Geoscientists and Oceanographers support natural-resource development, environmental protection, climate research, and offshore exploration across government agencies, universities, and industrial operations in Canada. Classified under NOC 21102, this TEER 1 professional occupation continues to see steady nationwide demand—creating strong immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Conduct geological, geochemical, and geophysical research
Plan and supervise field studies, drilling, and testing programs
Analyze survey data, well logs, and rock samples
Develop modelling software for subsurface analysis
Evaluate geological hazards and environmental risks
Advise on land acquisition and mine development
Conduct marine research programs and expeditions
Analyze seawater, sediments, plankton, and marine ecosystems
Model tides, currents, and sediment transport
Perform seabed surveys and offshore exploration studies
Study marine pollution and climate impacts
employment_requirements: University degree in geology, geochemistry, geophysics, or related fields
Master’s or doctoral degree for many research or geophysicist roles
Professional registration with provincial regulators (required in most regions)
Graduate degree in oceanography (often required for oceanographers)
Several years of supervised professional experience
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 10,100 new positions for Geoscientists and Oceanographers, while about 9,600 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and advanced-degree immigration planning especially valuable for overseas professionals.
additional_information: Geoscientists and Oceanographers remain in demand across petroleum, mining, environmental consulting, renewable-energy development, coastal research, and climate-science programs nationwide.

Petroleum and mining companiesGeological and geophysical consultanciesEnvironmental-engineering firmsGovernment research agenciesUniversities and marine-science institutesOffshore exploration and sea-farming enterprises
Natural-resource developmentEnvironmental remediation and monitoringOffshore-energy projectsClimate-change researchInfrastructure planning and hazard-risk mitigation
Annual salaries typically range between $57,100 and $144,300 CAD, depending on province, discipline, and seniority.
